content: Child health assessment includes: (1) Growth monitoring—WHO growth standards (0-5 years), CDC growth charts (2-20 years), BMI percentiles, head circumference (infants), growth velocity; (2) Developmental screening—Ages and Stages Questionnaire (ASQ), Modified Checklist for Autism in Toddlers (MCHAT), Denver Developmental Screening Test, surveillance at every visit; (3) Behavioral/emotional screening—Pediatric Symptom Checklist, PHQ-A for adolescents, SCARED for anxiety; (4) Vision/hearing screening—newborn hearing screening, photoscreening, audiometry at recommended ages; (5) Risk assessment—lead screening based on risk/housing age, tuberculosis testing, anemia screening, lipid screening (universal 9-11 years); (6) Population indicators—infant mortality rate, low birthweight prevalence, vaccination coverage (NIS-Child), developmental disability prevalence. Limitations include growth chart applicability across diverse populations, screening tool cultural validity, and fragmented data systems.
